scope: Verify status of all remaining A4 perf-hotspots P2/Low findings + Hypothesis test_composite deadlines
findings_assigned: A4#22 - fingerprint cache id() recycling risk
A5#4 / S51 - per-target select_target snapshots (sibling-handed to w10b)

A4#5.notes: Mis-tracked as DEFERRED in initial Wave-9 sweep; commit 5c6820ea (w4a follow-up) added `_ngb_fallback_snapshot` lazy-cache at module line 520 + companion `_cb_extra_fit_invariant` hoist for CB cat/text/embedding filter. Both hoists pinned by S47 sensor. FINAL_VERIFICATION updated by w10a.

A4#10.notes: `_build_disc_df_for_target` uses `filtered_train_df.assign(**{target_name: y_train_aligned})` on pandas + `.with_columns(pl.Series(...))` on polars - no `.copy(deep=False)` remains. Docstring at _phase_composite_discovery.py:37-50 documents the rationale.

A4#15.notes: Wave 9B (AP2) threaded precomputed _finite_mask through 9 residual _fit kernels. Verified composite_transforms.py:38 still has np.isfinite single-element check (inside @njit kernel body); top-level Python no longer recomputes.

A4#18.notes: _phase_composite_post.py:763-766 preallocates `np.empty((_n_rows, len(_oof_components)), dtype=np.float64)` and fills column-by-column instead of np.column_stack. The remaining column_stack at :459 is for `_b_cols` (base columns 0-2), not the K-component pred matrix - in scope of A4#18 only the latter.

A4#19.notes: _phase_composite_discovery.py:192-207 hoists group_ids coercion above the per-target loop with comment `Suite-constant group_ids: coerce once + materialise the filtered slice + length cap; the per-target loop below otherwise pays a fresh np.asarray + np.max per regression target`.

A4#20.notes: _phase_polars_fixes.py:229-239 only builds `_test_nulls_pre` under `if verbose and test_df_polars is not None and _eligible_cols:`. Single batched collect via lazy select replaces 2N per-col sync calls. Non-verbose runs pay nothing.

A4#21.notes: _composite_transforms_nonlinear.py:804+ uses bottleneck.move_median dispatcher (~8-10x pandas at k=7+). Reverse-window correction is built-in. Pandas fallback retained.

A4#22.notes: _phase_train_one_target_body.py:485-498 builds composite cache key as `(id(strategy), strategy.feature_tier(), strategy.supports_polars, pre_pipeline_name, _fp_train_df_id, _fp_train_df_ncols)`. The id(frame)+ncols pair catches the GC-recycle scenario (frame recycled at same address with same ncols is rare; pin via long-lived strong refs from ctx).

A4#23.notes: _main_train_suite.py:568-575 has explicit `elif isinstance(train_df_polars_pre, pl.DataFrame):` polars-fallback branch using `get_trainset_features_stats_polars(train_df_polars_pre)`. Comment at :569-571 documents the rationale (post-pandas-conversion frames can still leverage pinned polars original).

A5#4.notes: Per-target select_target snapshots are A5 caching cluster (not A4 perf-hotspots). Handed off to sibling agent w10b-caching-followups per task partition.

test_composite_hypothesis_deadlines.notes: Ran `pytest tests/training/test_composite.py::TestHypothesisRoundTrip --timeout=120`: all 3 Hypothesis property tests PASSED in 39.69s with deadline=2000ms and max_examples=30. No timeout failures observed - the prompt's `pre-existing Hypothesis health-check timeouts` concern is not reproducible. No action needed.
